title: E10-B12: v1.0 Release Cut
description: Version bump 1.0.0, CHANGELOG entry, git tag, GitHub Release, npm publication.

title: E10-B11: v1.0 Release Gate
description: Final release checklist: CI passes, evals pass, coverage targets met, docs updated, CHANGELOG complete, no critical beads open, user journey walkthrough, performance targets. Go/no-go.

title: Epic 10: Traces, Evals, Hardening, Remote Readiness, and v1 Gate
description: Make the system durable, inspectable, and ready for v1.0 release. Eval infrastructure, trace coverage, performance hardening, test coverage targets, npm package prep, release gate. Produces 12 beads.

title: Epic 9: Episodic Research, Stewardship, and Recall
description: Scoped research and human retention. ico research creates multi-agent task workspaces (Collector, Summarizer, Skeptic, Integrator). ico recall generates flashcards and quizzes. Produces 12 beads.
close_reason: Epic 9 complete: all 12 beads (B01–B12) shipped. Research pipeline (B01–B07) + Recall pipeline (B08–B11) + cross-feature integration tests (B12). 1053 tests passing.

title: Epic 8: Render, Promote, and Durable Artifact Operations
description: Turn answers into durable reusable assets. ico render report, ico render slides, and ico promote work. Artifacts are durable markdown files. Promotion enforces all 7 rules and detects 3 anti-patterns. Produces 10 beads.
close_reason: All 11 beads closed. 858 tests passing. Lint/typecheck clean. Render report/slides, promote/unpromote, artifact listing, post-promotion refresh, frontmatter validation all implemented.

title: Epic 7: Retrieval, Ask Flow, and Citation-Aware Answers
description: Make compiled knowledge queryable in a trustworthy way. ico ask returns sourced, traceable answers. Markdown-first retrieval (FTS5, no vector DB). Citation verification and provenance chain. Produces 10 beads.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic 5: Ingest Adapters and Source Identity
description: Get real source material into the system cleanly. Markdown, PDF, and web-clip sources with full metadata extraction, content hashing, and provenance tracking. Source-by-source and human-in-the-loop by default. Produces 10 beads.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic 6: Knowledge Compiler Core
description: Transform corpus into compiled semantic knowledge. All six compiler passes (Summarize, Extract, Synthesize, Link, Contradict, Gap), compiled pages conform to frontmatter schemas, ico compile is operational. Produces 12 beads.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic 4: CLI Surface and Operator Workflow
description: Make the system operable through a serious CLI. After this epic, the operator can run ico init, ico mount, ico ingest, ico status, and see real output. The CLI has proper error handling, help text, and formatted output. Produces 10 beads.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic 3: Kernel Core — Workspace, State, Mounts, and Provenance
description: Build the deterministic substrate. After this epic, the system can initialize workspaces, manage SQLite state, register mounts, track provenance, and enforce lifecycle rules. No AI calls — purely the deterministic side of the boundary. Produces 11 beads.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic 2: Repo Foundation, Packages, and Local Runtime Skeleton
description: Create the real monorepo/package baseline. After this epic, the repo has real TypeScript packages that build, lint, typecheck, and test. The stub scripts are gone. Produces 12 beads: pnpm workspace, kernel/cli/compiler/types packages, ESLint, Vitest, tsup, fixtures, CI upgrade, config loading, structured logger.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic 1: Canonical Design Pack, Standards, and Execution Templates
description: Lock the repo canon before implementation fans out. Every later epic references these artifacts for schema definitions, coding conventions, bead workflow, and quality standards. Produces 15 beads: frontmatter schemas, DB schema, trace schema, workspace policy, coding standards, bead conventions, testing strategy, CI/CD spec, prompt templates, promotion spec, CLAUDE.md update, exit review, glossary, ADR/AAR templates, diagram prompts.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Cognitive Workspace Protocol — Adversarial Review & Implementation
description: Phase A-F: Run 7-decade adversarial panel, synthesize findings, implement procfs kernel module, draft essay/spec/MCP design, produce formal report
close_reason: Delivered: procfs kernel module (13 tests), CLI integration, 7-decade adversarial review, essay draft, CWP spec v0.1, MCP server design, test audit. PR #11 created.

title: E7-B01: Full-Text Search over Compiled Pages
description: Implement FTS5-based full-text search over compiled wiki pages
notes: STRATEGIC NOTE (2026-04-06): Search interface as adapter pattern. Ship FTS5 adapter as default (dependency-free). Note qmd (BM25+vector+reranking, 18.2K stars, local-first, already has MCP server) as v1.1 upgrade path. Decision point at E7 start: evaluate qmd hybrid search vs raw FTS5. The search interface (searchPages, findRelevantPages) is the contract — the implementation behind it can be swapped. FTS5 is adapter #1; qmd is adapter #2. Users will hit the FTS5 ceiling on large corpora (50% probability); the adapter pattern ensures seamless upgrade.
close_reason: Duplicate of 4t6.1

title: E6-B09: Staleness Detection and Recompilation
description: Implement staleness detection for compiled pages and trigger recompilation when sources change
notes: STRATEGIC NOTE (2026-04-06): Architecture supports both incremental and full recompilation. If model costs drop 10x (60% probability by Q4 2026), shift recompilation strategy from incremental to full — recompile entire corpus on every source change. The staleness detection mechanism should be configurable: threshold-based (incremental, default) vs trigger-all (full recompile mode). Design the interface to support both without code changes.
close_reason: detectStalePages (source-changed + already-stale), markStale, getUncompiledSources. 12 tests.

title: E6-B01: Claude API Client Wrapper
description: Build the Claude API client wrapper for knowledge compilation passes
notes: STRATEGIC NOTE (2026-04-06): Use token-efficient tool calling mode (GA as of March 2026) for cheaper compilation passes. This reduces per-pass cost significantly. If model costs drop 10x (60% probability by Q4 2026), compilation becomes nearly free — but compilation remains necessary for durability, auditability, and accumulated knowledge regardless of cost. Cheap models make compilation affordable; they don't make it unnecessary.
close_reason: Duplicate of hrs.1 (E6-B01) which is already closed. Claude API client wrapper is implemented.

title: E3-B11: Kernel Package Barrel Export and API Surface Review
description: Review and finalize the kernel package public API surface for clean barrel exports
notes: STRATEGIC NOTE (2026-04-06): API surface must be wrappable as MCP tools without modification. This is the critical design constraint for v1.1 MCP integration. MCP is becoming the universal agent protocol (80% probability already happening). Every major IDE, LLM provider, and tool is adopting it. If the kernel API is clean, MCP wrapping is trivial. If messy, it's a rewrite. Acceptance criteria addition: 'every exported function can be described as a single MCP tool with typed input/output.'
close_reason: Clean barrel export: 30 functions + 7 types. initWorkspace/WorkspaceInfo added (was missing). initDatabaseWithMigrations removed from public surface. 11 barrel tests.

title: E1-B04: Workspace Directory Policy Document
description: Define workspace directory structure, naming conventions, and policy constraints
notes: STRATEGIC NOTE (2026-04-06): Obsidian compatibility constraint — output must be standard markdown + YAML frontmatter only. No ICO-specific extensions. Obsidian is the inspection surface; ICO is the compilation layer. This ensures interop with the largest local knowledge tool ecosystem and prevents architectural coupling to any single viewer.
close_reason: Duplicate of 0cf.5 (E1-B04). Canonical bead is 0cf.5, closed with workspace policy.

title: E1-B03: JSONL Trace Event Schema and Envelope Format
description: Define the canonical JSONL trace event schema and envelope format for L6 audit layer
notes: STRATEGIC NOTE (2026-04-06): Design traces for regulatory compliance. Hash chain must be legally defensible. EU AI Act enforcement Aug 2026 requires provenance for AI-generated content in high-risk domains. California and Colorado follow. ICO's L6 is a first-mover moat — no competitor has this built in from day one. Traces are not just for debugging; they are the shared evidence base for compliance, context refinement, and harness improvement.
close_reason: Duplicate of 0cf.4 (E1-B03). Canonical bead is 0cf.4, closed with trace schema.

title: E1-B15: v1 Scope Constraints and Security Standards
description: Combined security and scope standards: prompt injection defense (content-delimiting envelope), API key redaction policy, SQL injection prevention, path traversal/symlink policy, filename sanitization, file size limits, concurrency policy (WAL + lockfile), npm name verification, dependency audit policy, v1 feature deferrals (URL ingest, charts, vector search, remote). Produces 000-docs/021-AT-SECV-security-and-scope.md.
notes: STRATEGIC NOTE (2026-04-06): MCP server architecture — design kernel functions as callable via MCP, not just CLI. qmd (18.2K stars, BM25+vector+reranking, already has MCP server) as preferred retrieval engine candidate for v1.1. MCP is production-ready (Pinterest: 66K requests/month). Include competitive positioning statement: 'Karpathy's tool compiles knowledge. ICO governs it.' Never depend on session memory for durable state — all state must flow through the deterministic kernel. If Claude ships native persistent memory, ICO's value is compiled knowledge (deterministic, system-owned), not session memory (probabilistic, model-owned).
close_reason: 000-docs/021-AT-SECV-security-and-scope.md committed (e0f695f). 13 sections, all security rules with do/don't, 11 feature deferrals.

title: E10-B10: npm Package Preparation
description: Publication-ready package.json. ico binary from global install. prepublishOnly runs build+test+lint. npm pack + tarball install verification.

title: E10-B09: Test Coverage Gap Closure
description: Coverage analysis across all packages. Write tests to reach targets: kernel 90%, compiler 80%, cli 70%, types 100%. Focus on state machine, promotion, provenance, search, validation.

title: E10-B08: Status Document Update
description: Update 000-docs/006-OD-STAT-status.md: current phase, features, real metrics (coverage, CI rate, eval scores), decision log, risk assessment.
close_reason: E10-B08 shipped: 000-docs/006-OD-STAT-status.md rewritten from 2026-04-06 (Epic 1 docs-only) state to current 2026-05-16 (5 Epic 10 beads shipped) state. Real metrics throughout: 1107 test count broken out per-package, actual coverage % from pnpm test:coverage (kernel 83.2/90 target, compiler 82.3/80 ✓, cli 55.9/70 target, all-files 77.6), 13-entry release history, 137-bead ledger, full operator-visible CLI surface (18 commands with trace events per command), risk matrix with severity + mitigation + status per risk. Decision log captures 7 v1-relevant calls since standards freeze. Path to v1.0 graph + remaining-effort estimate. Suite stays 1107 green; docs-only.

title: E10-B07: CLAUDE.md Final Update
description: Remove pre-implementation language. Document all working CLI commands. Update architecture for deviations. Add Session Operations section. Troubleshooting tips.
close_reason: E10-B07 shipped: README.md rewritten — accurate quick-start with init step, complete CLI surface table (18 commands including new recall/eval surface), architecture table with six layers + path mapping, roadmap phases. CLAUDE.md additions: Session Operations section (workspace inspection, trace-based context refinement per audit M8, recall loop, env vars), Troubleshooting table (12 common symptoms → fixes). Current-state line updated to v0.15.1 with Epic 10 progress (B01/B04/B05/B07 done). Docs-only; suite stays 1107 green.

title: E10-B06: Performance Profiling
description: Profile against targets: ingest <2s, compile topic <30s, ask <10s, render report <5s, lint <30s. Identify bottlenecks. Optimize FTS5, SQLite, file I/O, prompts. Before/after measurements.

title: E10-B05: Error Handling Hardening
description: Review all error paths: user-friendly messages everywhere, no unhandled promise rejections, SQLite transactions, file system error handling, Claude API error specificity.
close_reason: E10-B05 shipped: top-level installProcessHandlers() catches uncaughtException, unhandledRejection, SIGINT (exits 130). friendlyError() lib maps Node fs errno (ENOSPC/EACCES/EROFS/ENOENT/EISDIR/EMFILE/EBUSY), SQLite (BUSY/READONLY/CORRUPT), and Claude API category errors to actionable messages. Mount add/remove emit audit-log entries (closes B04 gap). Disk-full simulation test (chmod 0o500 + atomic write) proves no .tmp/final files leak on filesystem failure. 19 new tests; suite 1107 green; lint + typecheck clean.

title: E10-B04: Trace Coverage Audit
description: Audit all commands for trace coverage. Walk through every CLI command verifying: trace emitted, schema conformance, correlation_id grouping. Fix gaps. Report.
notes: STRATEGIC NOTE (2026-04-06): Trace audit is a compliance gate, not just a quality check. EU AI Act enforcement Aug 2026 requires provenance for AI-generated content in high-risk domains. California and Colorado follow. Every output must be traceable to every input. Consider adding 'compliance mode' flag that enforces strict provenance. This bead is the legal defensibility gate for v1 — treat it with the same rigor as the release gate (E10-B11).
close_reason: E10-B04 shipped: trace coverage report at 000-docs/023-OD-AUDIT-trace-coverage-2026-05-15.md enumerates trace+audit-log emission per CLI command. Gap fix: ico lint now emits lint.run/lint.result traces per 011-AT-TRSC §6.19-6.20 (was previously missing). Integration test trace-coverage.integration.test.ts asserts lint, eval, and audit-chain integrity hold end-to-end. Read-only commands (status/inspect/recall weak/recall export) intentionally don't trace — documented. 4 new tests; suite 1088 green.

title: E10-B03: Retrieval and Citation Evals
description: Eval specs for ask flow: retrieval accuracy (recall@5, precision@5), citation fidelity, answer grounding. Fixture questions with known answers. evals/retrieval-citation/.

title: E10-B02: Compilation Quality Evals
description: Eval specs for compiler passes: Summarize (key claims?), Extract (right concepts?), Synthesize (covers sources?). 5+ criteria scored 1-5. evals/compilation-quality/.

title: E10-B01: Eval Framework
description: Implement ico eval run and eval framework. YAML spec format: eval name, type, input, expected outputs, scoring rubric. Runner loads specs, executes, reports results. evals/ directory with samples.
close_reason: E10-B01 shipped: packages/kernel/src/evals/ with loader, runner, retrieval + smoke handlers. YAML spec format validated on load. evals/ directory with 3 sample specs (2 smoke + 1 retrieval). ico eval run discovers + executes specs, emits eval.run/eval.result traces per 011-AT-TRSC §6.17-6.18 with shared correlation_id. 28 new tests; suite 1084 green; lint + typecheck clean.

title: E9-B12: Research and Recall Integration Tests
description: E2E tests: (1) full research pipeline, (2) recall card generation, (3) quiz scoring and retention tracking.
close_reason: E9-B12 shipped: packages/compiler/src/recall/recall-pipeline.integration.test.ts — end-to-end test for B08→B09→B10→B11 on one workspace. Covers generate cards from seeded wiki → quiz scoring → retention aggregation → Anki export. Also tests staleness signal (source_pages frontmatter preserved) and multi-session retention accumulation. 3 new integration tests; suite 1053 green; lint + typecheck clean. Epic 9 complete.

title: E9-B07: Research Task Archival
description: archiveTask() transitions completed->archived. Directory preserved but marked. ico research archive <taskId>. Non-completed task rejected.
close_reason: archiveTask() shipped in PR #30. Validates completed status, transitions to archived via kernel state machine, preserves full task directory, appends audit log. CLI: ico research archive <taskId> (research command restructured to command group, backward-compatible create). Tests: +6 (happy path with timestamp + directory preservation, reject created/collecting/nonexistent/already-archived). Full suite 964 passing, lint + typecheck clean.

title: E9-B06: Research Orchestrator
description: executeResearch() sequences Collector->Summarizer->Skeptic->Integrator. Error handling per stage. Copies final output to workspace/outputs/. Transitions to completed.
close_reason: Orchestrator shipped in PR #29. Implements executeResearch() sequencing Collector → Summarizer → Skeptic → Integrator → renderReport with: resume-aware starting stage, --step mode (confirmStep hook, default-abort when none supplied), ICO_MAX_RESEARCH_TOKENS budget (abort trace on overage, task stays in post-stage status so retry can resume), four new recoverable failure states in the kernel state machine (failed_collecting/_synthesizing/_critiquing/_rendering) with forward + recovery edges, migration 003 widens tasks.status CHECK constraint, retry:true rolls back failed_* → predecessor before resuming, L3→L4 handoff via gatherTaskOutput + renderReport (Integrator never touches L4), full trace coverage (orchestrator.start/.stage_start/.stage_complete/.retry/.pause/.abort/.complete). Tests: +5 kernel failure-transition cases, +12 orchestrator integration tests. Full suite 958 passing, lint + typecheck clean. CLI wiring deferred to follow-up.

title: E9-B05: Integrator Agent
description: integrateFindings() reads notes + critique, synthesizes final answer addressing critique. Writes to output/. Transitions to rendering.
close_reason: Integrator agent shipped in PR #28 (merged). packages/compiler/src/agents/integrator.ts implements integrateFindings() — reads brief + notes/synthesis.md + critique/critique.md, calls Claude with mandate to address every critique concern and end with 'How this answer addresses the critique' section preserving inline [source: <title>] citations, writes output/final.md atomically with frontmatter (task_id, integrated_at, model, notes_path, critique_path, tokens), emits notes.integrate trace, transitions critiquing→rendering. Per E9 blueprint: L3→L4 handoff belongs to the Orchestrator (E9-B06), not this agent. 11 integration tests (happy path + 6 error paths). Gemini COMMENTED with 4 false-positive comments about backslashes in prompts (diff-escaping artefact — actual source has no backslashes; verified + dismissed). All 942 tests pass (types 13 + kernel 240 + compiler 382 + cli 306). Lint + typecheck clean. Marks all 4 per-agent beads of Epic 9 complete; Orchestrator (E9-B06) is now unblocked.

title: E9-B04: Skeptic Agent
description: critiqueFindings() reads notes, identifies weak evidence, unsupported claims, missing perspectives, logical gaps. Writes to critique/. Transitions to critiquing.
close_reason: Skeptic agent shipped in PR #27 (merged). packages/compiler/src/agents/skeptic.ts implements critiqueFindings() — reads notes/synthesis.md, calls Claude with adversarial four-section system prompt (Weak Evidence / Unsupported Claims / Missing Perspectives / Logical Gaps), writes critique/critique.md atomically with frontmatter (task_id, critiqued_at, model, notes_path, tokens), emits notes.critique trace, transitions synthesizing→critiquing. 10 integration tests (happy path + 5 error paths including API error leaves task in synthesizing). Gemini COMMENTED, one inline suggestion applied (exact transition count assertion); rejected date-injection suggestion for inter-agent consistency (collector + summarizer use same pattern). All 931 tests pass (types 13 + kernel 240 + compiler 371 + cli 306). Lint + typecheck clean.

title: E9-B03: Summarizer Agent
description: summarizeEvidence() reads evidence files, synthesizes into coherent working notes in notes/. Transitions to synthesizing.
close_reason: Summarizer agent shipped in PR #26 (merged). packages/compiler/src/agents/summarizer.ts implements summarizeEvidence(db, wsPath, taskId, client, opts) — reads task brief and evidence/, calls injected ClaudeClient with XML-delimited prompt + injection defense in system message + [source: <title>] citation format matching ask/generate.ts, writes consolidated notes/synthesis.md atomically (.tmp+rename) with frontmatter (task_id, synthesized_at, model, evidence_count, input/output/total tokens, source_paths list), emits evidence.synthesize trace, transitions task collecting→synthesizing. 13 integration tests (happy path, frontmatter robustness for JSON-quoted numeric values, 5 error paths including Claude API error leaves task in collecting, E2E integration with real Collector output). Design: one consolidated synthesis.md (not per-evidence) — spec calls for 'coherent working notes' and downstream Skeptic/Integrator need single narrative. Gemini review COMMENTED, addressed Medium-severity JSON.parse cast bug via String() coercion + regression test. All 920 tests pass (types 13 + kernel 240 + compiler 361 + cli 306). Lint + typecheck clean.

title: E9-B02: Collector Agent
description: collectEvidence() searches compiled knowledge for relevant pages, copies excerpts to tasks/<id>/evidence/ with source citations. Transitions to collecting.
notes: PR #24 merged to main. Commit 43f5cbd squashed. Also merged PR #25 (chore/ci-replace-retired-npm-audit-endpoint) which fixed environmental Security Audit failure caused by npm retiring /-/npm/v1/security/audits on 2026-04-15; replaced with google/osv-scanner-action. All 4 CI checks green on merge.
close_reason: Implemented collectEvidence() in packages/compiler/src/agents/collector.ts. Reads task brief.md, builds stop-word-filtered OR-joined FTS5 query, searches compiled wiki, writes evidence files to tasks/tsk-<id>/evidence/NN-<slug>.md with frontmatter citing source_path/source_title/source_type/rank/collected_at/truncated. Emits evidence.collect trace per file, transitions task created→collecting (emits task.transition trace). Truncates bodies >4000 chars. Pure deterministic — no Claude API. 16 integration tests (happy path: evidence shape/frontmatter/transition/ordering/traces; options: maxResults cap and maxExcerptChars truncation; 6 error paths: missing task, wrong status, missing brief.md, empty brief, unsearchable brief, zero matches; idempotency). All 907 tests pass (types 13 + kernel 240 + compiler 348 + cli 306). Lint + typecheck clean. PR #24 https://github.com/jeremylongshore/intentional-cognition-os/pull/24. Commit 9e7077a on feat/epic9-collector-agent-bzbk8-2.

title: E9-B01: Research Task Creation
description: Implement ico research <brief>. Creates task workspace: ID (task-YYYYMMDD-NNN), directories (evidence/, notes/, drafts/, critique/, output/), SQLite record, brief.md, trace, audit log.
close_reason: Already implemented and shipped in commit 0813732 (PR #10, v0.4.0). packages/cli/src/commands/research.ts creates task workspace with evidence/notes/drafts/critique/output dirs via kernel.createTask, writes brief.md with YAML frontmatter (task_id, created_at, status), registers SQLite row with status='created', emits task.create trace event, appends research.create audit log entry. Supports --json and --workspace global flags. research.test.ts covers result shape, brief.md content, audit log, human/JSON output, error paths, DB cleanup, plus integration tests against real kernel (directory structure, SQLite status, trace events, audit log). All 306 CLI tests passing. Bead was left open after merge — closing now.

title: Install @intentsolutions/audit-harness v0.1.0 testing baseline
description:
ICOS is the highest-leverage Tier 1 repo for testing-SOP adoption per the 2026-05-14 HARNESS-SYNC-REPORT.md. It's at v0.11.0 with active Epic 9 work but has zero audit-tests issues filed and no harness installed.

Installing the harness here is the first step toward replicating the qmd-team-intent-kb audit-tests playbook (14 open issues at https://github.com/jeremylongshore/qmd-team-intent-kb/issues?q=is%3Aopen+label%3Aaudit-tests) in ICOS, which is required before Epic 16 (ICOS->qmd memory bridge tracked at qmd-team-intent-kb-01x) can ship.

Step 1 of 3:
  1. Install harness, push feature branch + PR  <-- THIS BEAD
  2. Run /audit-tests to produce TEST_AUDIT.md (read-only)
  3. File audit-tests issues mirroring qmd-team-intent-kb's labeling taxonomy
acceptance_criteria: .audit-harness/ vendored at repo root. scripts/audit-harness wrapper executable. scripts/audit-harness verify exits 0. PR opened against main on a feat/install-audit-harness-baseline branch.
close_reason: Harness install was already shipped via PR #38 (merged between v0.11.0 and v0.11.1 on remote main). My local checkout was 3 commits behind origin so the /sync-testing-harness classifier saw NOT_INSTALLED. After git pull --ff-only the .audit-harness/ tree + scripts/audit-harness wrapper are present at v0.1.0. No action needed; ICOS reclassified as CURRENT.

description: ico recall quiz. Interactive: present questions, accept answers, AI-score short answers, record in recall_results table. Final score and weak areas.

title: E9-B08: Recall Card Generator
description: ico recall generate --topic <name>. Read compiled knowledge, generate flashcards (Q/A) and quiz questions. Save to recall/cards/ and recall/quizzes/ with source references.
close_reason: E9-B08 shipped: generateRecall() in packages/compiler/src/recall/generate.ts produces flashcards + quiz from FTS5-matched wiki pages via Claude. CLI: ico recall generate --topic <name>. Trace: recall.generate per 011-AT-TRSC §6.14. Frontmatter records source_pages for staleness detection (audit M7). 30 new tests (compiler + cli); full suite 988 green; lint + typecheck clean.

title: Zod 3.24 → 4.x migration
description: Zod 4 has breaking changes in schema parsing, error formats, and some API renames (.parse/.safeParse behavior, refinements, brand types). Touches packages/types/src/schemas.ts (frontmatter schemas, TaskStatus, trace envelope) and every consumer. Needs a real audit pass. Schedule after Epic 9 ships.

title: TypeScript 5.8 → 6.x migration
description: Major version bump across workspace (packages/types, kernel, compiler, cli, plus root devDep). Requires real code changes — stricter type inference, some breaking changes in import/export syntax and decorator behavior. Out of scope for dependabot auto-merge. Schedule after Epic 9 ships so we're not moving ground during active agent work.
